title: Verify that if defined, readOnlyPort is set to 0

input_row.title: Verify that if defined, readOnlyPort is set to 0
input_row.rationale: The Kubelet process provides a read-only API in addition to the main Kubelet API. Unauthenticated access is provided to this read-only API which could possibly retrieve potentially sensitive information about the cluster. Impact: Removal of the read-only port will require that any service which made use of it will need to be re-configured to use the main Kubelet API.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet Verify that the --read-only-port argument exists and is set to 0. If the --read-only-port argument is not present, check that there is a Kubelet config file specified by --config. Check that if there is a readOnlyPort entry in the file, it is set to 0.
input_row.remediation: If using a Kubelet config file, edit the file to set readOnlyPort to 0. If using command line arguments, edit the kubelet service file /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf on each worker node and set the below parameter in KUBELET_SYSTEM_PODS_ARGS variable. --read-only-port=0 Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart kubelet.service References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/6cedc0853faa118df0ba3d41b48b 993422ad3df6/staging/src/k8s.io/kubelet/config/v1beta1/types.go#L142 Additional Information: https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kubelet/
input_row.impact: Removal of the read-only port will require that any service which made use of it will need to be re-configured to use the main Kubelet API.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions checking the `--read-only-port` argument for the Kubelet, which can be verified through the Kubelet's configuration.
- The Kubelet configuration can be accessed via the Kubernetes API or through `kubectl` commands.
- The requirement to check if the `readOnlyPort` is set to 0 can be automated by querying the Kubelet's configuration.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_read_only_port_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the following command to check the Kubelet configuration:
  ```bash
  kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.nodeInfo.kubeletVersion}'
  ```
- Additionally, check the Kubelet command line arguments using:
  ```bash
  ps -ef | grep kubelet
  ```
- If a Kubelet configuration file is specified, check the contents of that file for the `readOnlyPort` setting.
- The expected value for `readOnlyPort` should be `0`. If the `--read-only-port` argument is present, it should also be set to `0`.
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - If `--read-only-port` is not present, or if it is present and set to `0`, the check passes.
  - If `readOnlyPort` in the Kubelet config file is set to `0`, the check passes.
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - If `--read-only-port` is present and not set to `0`, or if `readOnlyPort` in the Kubelet config file is not set to `0`, the check fails.
